actions.value:
Thanks for reaching out and am very sorry to hear about what happened, and we have some questions to try to find out how this may have happened.


First, when creating an Edge account, everything is completely encrypted before being backed up, meaning not even Edge can see the username, password, or the transactions inside the wallet.

If you think your account is compromised, please IMMEDIATELY STOP USING IT AND DO NOT RECEIVE ANY ADDITIONAL FUNDS AS MORE CAN BE LOST.


With that said, here are some questions below. Please fill the replies in the form here https://form.asana.com/?k=g4MFFV8sJJC6P-nxQb192Q&d=9976422036640


1) Did you ever store your username/password in an email, cloud, or have it physically written down somewhere?

2) Have you ever signed into another device?

3) Does anyone else such as family or friends have access to the device or account?

4) Have you ever revealed the Master Private Seed or private keys of the wallet and written it down somewhere or save it in an email or cloud?

5) Is your username and password the same as some other websites or social media that you use?

6) Do you remember ever doing a Slide to Confirm send around the time that transaction was made which you do not recognize?

7) If you go to https://haveibeenpwned.com/ and enter your email address there, does it indicate that your email has been leaked at any point?

8) Did you have 2FA enabled on your account? This would have prevented any unknown logins from any device except your own physical device

9) Was your account initially created on the Edge app or some other app such as Airbitz or Bitcoin of America etc.?

10) Was the account completely EMPTIED out, meaning you have 0 or very close to 0 balance? Or do you still have a significant balance left in the account?

​11) Lastly, what is the username of the account? And what city/state are you in? With this information, we can look up your account history to see if anyone has accessed the account from outside your location
